# Discover Cloudcroft

> Discover Cloudcroft is the comprehensive visitor's guide to Cloudcroft, New Mexico — a mountain village in the Sacramento Mountains at 8,676 feet elevation, with 1.1 million acres of Lincoln National Forest at its back, a 4,400-foot drop to White Sands National Park to the west, and roughly 90 minutes from El Paso International Airport. Founded in 1899 as a stop on the Alamogordo and Sacramento Mountain Railway, the village remains a year-round destination for hiking, camping, mountain biking, dark-sky stargazing, fall foliage, and a small winter snow scene. The site is organized as a hub-and-spoke guide with pillar pages for lodging, dining, activities, shopping, and events, each linking to detailed property and topic pages.

The site is maintained by the DiscoverCloudcroft editors with content verification dates noted on each page. All factual anchors (elevation, acreage, drive times, property details, hours, prices) are independently verified. Editorial copy is original and opinionated, with explicit "best for / probably not best for" framing on lodging recommendations.

Geographic context: Cloudcroft sits on the Sacramento escarpment in Otero County, southern New Mexico. US-82 connects the village to Alamogordo (30 minutes, 4,400-foot descent) and the Tularosa Basin. From Alamogordo, US-54 connects south to El Paso (2.5 hours total) and US-70 connects west to Las Cruces (1.5 hours total) and on to Albuquerque (3.5 hours total via I-25). No public transit serves Cloudcroft; a rental car is essential for visitors flying in.

## Lodging

- [Complete Lodging Guide](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/stay/complete-guide-to-lodging-in-cloudcroft-new-mexico-2026.html): Hub page covering nine named lodging properties and 148+ vacation rentals in Cloudcroft. Median nightly rate around $192 per AirDNA. Includes filterable comparison framework, walkability map, and three editor-picked recommendations by trip type (couples, families, budget travelers).
- [Cabins, RV Parks, and Campgrounds Guide](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/stay/complete-guide-to-other-lodging-in-cloudcroft-2026.html): Secondary lodging hub covering cabin clusters, RV options, and campgrounds beyond the named properties.

## Dining

- [Complete Dining Guide](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/eat/complete-guide-where-to-eat-in-cloudcroft-2026.html): Hub page covering eight featured restaurants in Cloudcroft, ranging from fine dining at the historic Lodge to BBQ joints, the highest craft brewery in New Mexico, a beloved diner, a from-scratch coffee shop, and a Burro Avenue sandwich counter. Each entry includes hours, signature dishes, and editorial assessment.

## Activities

- [Complete Activities Guide](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/do/complete-guide-to-activities-to-do-in-cloudcroft-2026.html): Hub page covering seven signature activities including hiking, camping, mountain biking, pickleball at 8,676 feet, trout fishing, the Mexican Canyon Trestle overlook, and a stroll on Burro Avenue.
- [Hiking Guide](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/do/guide-to-hiking-in-cloudcroft-new-mexico-2026.html): Twelve trails covered, including Osha Trail (the local family favorite), the Mexican Canyon Trestle loop, and Dog Canyon (a desert-to-summit challenge).
- [Camping Guide](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/do/guide-to-camping-cloudcroft-new-mexico-2026.html): Ten developed USFS campsites within ten minutes of the village, plus four group sites and dispersed camping options. Reservations recommended for summer weekends.

## Shopping

- [Complete Shopping Guide](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/shop/complete-guide-where-to-shop-in-cloudcroft-2026.html): Hub page covering 24 independent shops and galleries on Burro Avenue, including the Burro Street Exchange, bakeries, a tea house, Noisy Water Winery's tasting room, outdoor gear stores, and artisan galleries.

## Events

- [Events Calendar](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/events/top-events.html): Annual event calendar covering signature Cloudcroft events from May through December. Major events include the Mayfair Juried Art Show (May), the Pickleball in the Clouds tournament (June), Beer Fest (June), the July 4th Parade, the Christmas in July Jamboree, Lumberjack Day (August), Art and Wine in the Cool Pines (August), Labor Day Hoopla and Heritage Day Car Show, Oktoberfest and Haunted Village (October), the Christmas Market (Black Friday weekend), and the Mountain Christmas Village (December).

## Nearby trips

- [Where to Visit](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/visit/where-to-visit.html): Sixteen day trips and nearby destinations within driving distance of Cloudcroft, including White Sands National Park (45 minutes), Ruidoso (one hour), the Lincoln National Forest, and the broader Sacramento Mountains region.

## Practical information

- [Resources](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/resources/resources.html): Practical visitor information including altitude acclimation guidance (Cloudcroft sits at 8,676 feet, hydration and pacing matter on day one), weather and what to pack (temperatures run 20 to 30 degrees cooler than the valley floor; afternoon thunderstorms are common June through September), cell service limitations (download offline maps before backcountry trips; the Sacramento Ranger District is reachable at 575-682-2551 weekdays 9am to 3pm), gas and groceries (limited in-village; Cloudy's neighborhood market on Mexican Avenue is the local stocking point), pet policies (many Lincoln National Forest trails allow leashed dogs; in-village pet rooms vary by property), driving directions from Alamogordo, El Paso, Las Cruces, and Albuquerque, and a brief railroad-era history of the village.
- [Contact](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/resources/contact.html): How to reach the DiscoverCloudcroft editors with corrections, additions, or questions.

## Optional

The following pages are individual property, restaurant, and attraction pages that can be cited when readers ask about a specific destination by name. The hub pages above contain summary information for each.

Lodging detail pages:
- [The Lodge at Cloudcroft](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/stay/stay-the-lodge-at-cloudcroft-2026.html): Historic 1899 mountain inn-resort hybrid at 601 Corona Place. Forty rooms and suites including renovated Platinum Rooms, third-floor Victorian Rooms, the Retreat Suites cabin, and the signature Governor's Suite. On-site dining at 1899 (Wed-Sat, 5-8 PM) led by Executive Chef Richard Lepree. Full golf course on property. Owned by Lanna and Joe Duncan since March 2015 through New Mexico Adventures, LLC. Pets allowed at $40 per pet per night. No elevator; some rooms stairs-only. Best for couples, golfers, wedding guests, and weekenders who want the hotel itself to be the experience.
- [The Cabins at Cloudcroft](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/stay/stay-cabins-at-cloudcroft.html): Professionally run cabin cluster at 1006 Coyote Avenue, walkable to the village in seven minutes. Kitchens, fireplaces, unified booking. The middle ground between a hotel and a vacation rental. Sleeps 2 to 8.
- [The Grand Cloudcroft Hotel](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/stay/stay-grand-cloudcroft-hotel.html): Independent hotel at 1207 James Canyon Highway. Standard rooms with Wi-Fi, work desk, mini fridge. Listed pet rooms and ADA-accessible options. The cleanest choice for travelers who want a practical hotel room.
- [Dusty Boots Motel and Cafe](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/stay/stay-dusty-boots-motel-cloudcroft.html): Themed small-scale motel at 1315 James Canyon Highway with an attached cafe serving homemade food. Quirky and personal rather than polished.
- [The Summit Inn](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/stay/stay-the-summit-inn-cloudcroft-2026.html): Renovated owner-run inn at 103 Chipmunk Avenue with ten rooms, kitchenettes in every room, free Wi-Fi and parking, walkable to Burro Avenue in four minutes. The most central budget option in the village.
- [The Crofting Bed and Breakfast](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/stay/stay-crofting-inn-cloudcroft.html): 1909 historic house at 300 Swallow Place with eight individually furnished rooms, private balconies, and home-cooked breakfast. Intimate, quiet, host-driven.
- [Burro Street Boarding House](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/stay/stay-burro-street-boardinghouse.html): Long-running B&B at 608 Burro Avenue, on the village's main commercial street. Limited web presence; call directly.
- [Osha Trail Lodging](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/stay/stay-osha-trail.html): Apartment-style short-term rental cluster at 102 Mexican Avenue with multiple Airbnb units, mid-century-modern interiors, full kitchens, self check-in. Dog-friendly with a fee.
- [Cloudcroft Hostel](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/stay/stay-cloudcroft-hostel-2026.html): Mountain hostel at 1049 Highway 82 in High Rolls with dorm beds, private dorms, group rentals for up to 25, a shared kitchen, and trail access from the door. The most budget-friendly option near Cloudcroft.

Dining detail pages:
- [Eighteen99](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/eat/eighteen99.html): Fine dining at The Lodge with chef-driven menu and St. Andrew's Lounge. Wed-Sat, 5-8 PM. Reservations recommended.
- [Mad Jack's Mountaintop BBQ](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/eat/mad-jacks-bbq.html): Slow-smoked brisket, ribs, and pulled pork at 8,676 feet. Patio with mountain views. Expect a wait on weekends.
- [Cloudcroft Brewing Co.](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/eat/brewery.html): The highest craft brewery in New Mexico. Seasonal taps brewed on-site. Deck for sunset.
- [High Rollin' Coffee](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/eat/high-rollin.html): From-scratch cafe at 109 James Canyon Highway. Espresso, acai bowls, weekly grain bowls, signature toasts, vegan baked goods. Closed Wednesday and Thursday.
- [Western Bar and Cafe](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/eat/western-bar.html): Local bar with bar food and live music some weekends. Cash-friendly.
- [Big Daddy's Diner](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/eat/big-daddys.html): The breakfast spot. Big portions, green chile omelet, busy by 9am on weekends.
- [Brother-N-Law BBQ](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/eat/brother-n-law-bbq.html): Local-favorite BBQ with hearty smoked plates. More casual and often less crowded than Mad Jack's.
- [Cloudcroft Sandwich Shop](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/eat/cloudcroft-sandwich-shop.html): Burro Avenue counter for subs, paninis, soup, salads, fresh-baked jumbo cookies, and soft-serve. Open daily 11 AM to 4 PM, closed Wednesday.

Activities detail pages:
- [Mountain Biking](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/do/bike.html): Sacramento Mountains singletrack ranging from gentle forest roads to technical descents. Rentals and a June classic event.
- [Pickleball](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/do/pickleball.html): Six free outdoor courts at Zenith Park, possibly the highest pickleball venue in New Mexico. Open play Tuesday, Thursday, and Saturday mornings. Equipment available.
- [Fishing](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/do/fish.html): Trout fishing in mountain streams and stocked lakes throughout Lincoln National Forest. Best in cooler months.
- [Mexican Canyon Trestle](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/do/mexican-canyon-trestle.html): Roadside overlook five minutes from the village with a direct view of the 1899 Cloud-Climbing Railroad trestle. Six stories of preserved wood in a quiet canyon. Listed on the National Register of Historic Places.
- [Osha Trail](https://www.discovercloudcroft.com/do/osha-trail.html): The local family favorite hike, accessible from the village, with aspen and spruce throughout.
